narration: Worst standing N-1 is loss of trafo 7 (bus 64 to bus 67) (band HIGH, cascade_depth 2).
Base case is within limits this interval, so no real-time redispatch is committed.
A base-case OPF cannot N-1-secure a standing contingency; that residual is the storage-siting question handled by the battery recommender, kept on watch.
Before the agent acted, 1 line were loaded past their thermal limit (worst at 100%), so the base case was insecure.
The redispatch did not reach a secure base: 1 violation cleared so far, but a residual remains and is reported, not hidden.

The proposed move shifts 598 MW across 25 generators (chiefly gen 38 (607 to 471 MW), gen 4 (450 to 388 MW)), at a cost of €14,463, and avoids 1,968 MW of shedding so far; it stays uncommitted until the AC cascade rescan shows the base fully secure; keep trafo_7 on watch as the nearest remaining constraint.
Before the agent acted, 1 line were loaded past their thermal limit (worst at 106%), so the base case was insecure.
Now the base case is secure: all 1 live violation cleared with nothing left over its limit, and the most-severe N-1 contingencies still hold with none worsened.
